lesson.menu_desc: Entertaining a client at a restaurant -- polite requests and recommendations -- 28 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 19 -- Out for Dinner -- Entertaining a Client at a Restaurant
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Walyson Ginaldo Silva | Aula 19 -- Out for Dinner

listenings.text: Hi Walyson, this is Clara again. Thank you so much for inviting me to dinner tonight; it is very kind of you to host me. I just wanted to mention a couple of things before we meet at the restaurant. I do not eat red meat, so I will probably go for fish or a vegetarian dish. Also, I am not very hungry after the long day, so I might just have a starter and a main course, no dessert. By the way, I trust your taste, so I would love a recommendation from you. You know the local food much better than I do. Please do not order anything too spicy for me. I look forward to a relaxed evening. See you at eight.

This is my note to prepare for the dinner with Clara tonight. As the host, my goal is to make her feel comfortable and well looked after. First, when we arrive, I will check that the table is ready and let her choose her seat. Then I will help her with the menu and offer a recommendation, because she trusts my taste. I will remember that she does not eat red meat, so I will suggest the grilled fish or a vegetarian option, and nothing too spicy. I will use polite requests with the waiter; instead of saying bring us water, I will say could you bring us some water, or would you mind bringing the menu. At the end, I will quietly ask for the bill and take care of it, because I invited her. A good dinner builds a strong relationship before we sign anything.

extra_audio.text: First, welcome your guest at the restaurant and let them choose a comfortable seat. Next, hand them the menu and offer a recommendation, especially if they trust your taste. After that, use polite requests with the waiter, for example could you bring us some water, instead of just bring water. Then, during the meal, make light conversation and check that your guest is enjoying the food. Finally, quietly ask for the bill and take care of it, because you are the host who invited the client.
